name: WooCommerce
shortDescription: Connect a self-hosted WooCommerce store to FlowRunner and automate your storefront over the WooCommerce REST API (`/wp-json/wc/v3`). Manage products, variations, categories, global attributes and their terms, orders, notes, refunds, customers, and coupons - bulk-sync products, orders, and customers in a single request, and react in real time to new and changed orders, products, and customers. Auth uses a per-store Consumer Key/Secret over HTTPS, with an optional query-string fallback for hosts that strip the Authorization header.

actions.name: Create Product
actions.description: Creates a new product in the store catalog. Use this to add inventory programmatically - a simple item, or a variable parent you then attach variations to.

actions.name: Get Product
actions.description: Retrieves a single product by ID, including price, stock, and category data. Pick the product from the dropdown instead of pasting an ID.

actions.name: List Products
actions.description: Lists products, optionally filtered by search text, category, or status. Use this to browse the catalog or find a product before acting on it.

actions.name: Update Product
actions.description: Updates an existing product's name, price, status, or any other field. Use this to change pricing, publish a draft, or adjust catalog details.

actions.name: Delete Product
actions.description: Deletes a product. By default it is moved to trash; enable Permanent to remove it entirely. Use when retiring inventory.

actions.name: Create Product Variation
actions.description: Adds a variation (e.g. a specific size/color combination) to a variable product. Use after creating a 'variable' product to define its purchasable options.

actions.name: Get Product Variation
actions.description: Retrieves a single variation of a variable product by ID, including its price and stock.

actions.name: List Product Variations
actions.description: Lists all variations of a variable product. Use to enumerate the purchasable options of a product.

actions.name: Update Product Variation
actions.description: Updates a variation's price, stock, or other fields. Use to reprice or restock a specific size/color.

actions.name: Delete Product Variation
actions.description: Permanently deletes a variation of a variable product. Variations cannot be trashed, so this removal is final.

actions.name: Create Product Category
actions.description: Creates a product category to organize the catalog. Optionally nest it under a parent category.

actions.name: Get Product Category
actions.description: Retrieves a single product category by ID, including its product count and parent.

actions.name: List Product Categories
actions.description: Lists product categories, optionally filtered by search text. Use to discover how the catalog is organized.

actions.name: Update Product Category
actions.description: Updates a product category's name or description.

actions.name: Delete Product Category
actions.description: Permanently deletes a product category. Categories cannot be trashed, so this removal is final; products are not deleted but lose this category.

actions.name: Create Product Attribute
actions.description: Creates a global product attribute (e.g. "Color" or "Size") that can be reused across products. Create this first, then add its terms (the selectable values) and assign it to a variable product before defining variations.

actions.name: Get Product Attribute
actions.description: Retrieves a single global product attribute by ID, including its type and sort order.

actions.name: List Product Attributes
actions.description: Lists the store's global product attributes, optionally filtered by search text. Use to discover which attributes exist before creating terms or assigning them to a variable product.

actions.name: Update Product Attribute
actions.description: Updates a global product attribute's name, slug, sort order, or archive setting.

actions.name: Delete Product Attribute
actions.description: Permanently deletes a global product attribute and all of its terms. Attributes cannot be trashed, so this removal is final; products that used it lose those attribute selections.

actions.name: Create Attribute Term
actions.description: Adds a selectable term (e.g. "Blue" for the "Color" attribute) to a global product attribute. Use after creating the attribute so it has values to assign to variable products.

actions.name: Get Attribute Term
actions.description: Retrieves a single term of a global product attribute by ID, including how many products use it.

actions.name: List Attribute Terms
actions.description: Lists the terms (selectable values) of a global product attribute, optionally filtered by search text. Use to see or reuse an attribute's existing options, e.g. all colors already defined.

actions.name: Update Attribute Term
actions.description: Updates a term's name, slug, description, or sort order within a global product attribute.

actions.name: Delete Attribute Term
actions.description: Permanently deletes a term from a global product attribute. Terms cannot be trashed, so this removal is final; variations using it lose that attribute selection.

actions.name: Create Order
actions.description: Creates an order with line items, customer, and billing details. Use to record a sale made outside the storefront or to draft an order for a customer.

actions.name: Get Order
actions.description: Retrieves a single order by ID, including line items, totals, and customer/billing data.

actions.name: List Orders
actions.description: Lists orders, optionally filtered by status, customer, or search text. Use to find recent sales or a customer's order history.

actions.name: Update Order
actions.description: Updates an order - most often to change its status (e.g. mark processing or completed). Use to advance fulfillment or correct order details.

actions.name: Delete Order
actions.description: Deletes an order. By default it is moved to trash; enable Permanent to remove it entirely.

actions.name: Create Order Note
actions.description: Adds a note to an order. Mark it as a customer note to email it to the buyer, or leave it private for staff. Use for fulfillment updates or internal remarks.

actions.name: Get Order Note
actions.description: Retrieves a single note on an order by ID.

actions.name: List Order Notes
actions.description: Lists all notes on an order, both customer-facing and private. Use to review an order's history.

actions.name: Delete Order Note
actions.description: Permanently deletes a note from an order. Notes cannot be trashed, so this removal is final.

actions.name: Create Order Refund
actions.description: Issues a refund against an order for a given amount. Optionally trigger the payment gateway to refund the customer automatically. Use for returns and chargebacks.

actions.name: Get Order Refund
actions.description: Retrieves a single refund on an order by ID.

actions.name: List Order Refunds
actions.description: Lists all refunds issued against an order. Use to total what has already been refunded.

actions.name: Delete Order Refund
actions.description: Permanently deletes a refund record from an order. This removes the record only; it does not reverse money already returned.

actions.name: Create Customer
actions.description: Creates a customer account. Use to register a buyer programmatically so future orders attach to a known profile.

actions.name: Get Customer
actions.description: Retrieves a single customer by ID, including billing/shipping and spend totals.

actions.name: List Customers
actions.description: Lists customers, optionally filtered by search text or an exact email. Use to look up a buyer before creating an order.

actions.name: Update Customer
actions.description: Updates a customer's profile fields, e.g. name or addresses.

actions.name: Delete Customer
actions.description: Permanently deletes a customer account. Customers cannot be trashed, so this removal is final; optionally reassign their posts to another user.

actions.name: Create Coupon
actions.description: Creates a discount coupon. Use to run promotions - a percentage off, a fixed cart discount, or a fixed product discount.

actions.name: Get Coupon
actions.description: Retrieves a single coupon by ID, including its discount type, amount, and usage count.

actions.name: List Coupons
actions.description: Lists coupons, optionally filtered by search text against the code. Use to audit active promotions.

actions.name: Update Coupon
actions.description: Updates a coupon's amount, description, or other fields. Use to adjust an ongoing promotion.

actions.name: Delete Coupon
actions.description: Deletes a coupon. By default it is moved to trash; enable Permanent to remove it entirely.

actions.name: Batch Products
actions.description: Creates, updates, and/or deletes multiple products in a single request. Use this to sync a catalog in bulk instead of calling Create/Update/Delete Product repeatedly. WooCommerce allows at most 100 items total across Create, Update, and Delete per request.

actions.name: Batch Orders
actions.description: Creates, updates, and/or deletes multiple orders in a single request. Use this to bulk-import orders or push status changes across many orders at once. WooCommerce allows at most 100 items total across Create, Update, and Delete per request.

actions.name: Batch Customers
actions.description: Creates, updates, and/or deletes multiple customers in a single request. Use this to bulk-import a customer list or update many profiles at once. WooCommerce allows at most 100 items total across Create, Update, and Delete per request.

idealUseCases: Sync products, prices, and stock between WooCommerce and another system (ERP, PIM, spreadsheet).
Automate order fulfillment: advance status, add notes, and issue refunds without leaving your flow.
Trigger workflows the moment an order is placed or updated - send confirmations, notify a warehouse, post to chat.
Onboard new customers to a CRM or mailing list automatically when they register or update their profile.
Run promotions by creating and adjusting discount coupons programmatically.
Define reusable global attributes (e.g. Color, Size) and their terms, then build variable products from them.
Bulk-import or reconcile large catalogs, order sets, or customer lists in one call with the batch actions.
Let an AI agent answer "what did this customer order?" using dictionary-backed pickers.
agentIdeas: When a **WooCommerce** "On Order Created" trigger fires, use **ShipBob** "Create Order" to push the sale into fulfillment, then **Gmail** "Send Message" to email the customer an order confirmation with the expected ship date.
When a **WooCommerce** "On Customer Created" trigger fires, use **Brevo** "Create Contact" to add the new buyer to your CRM and mailing list automatically.

When a **WooCommerce** "On Order Updated" trigger reports a cancelled order, chain **WooCommerce** "Create Order Refund" to return the funds and **Slack** "Send Message To Channel" to alert the finance team with the order ID and amount.
